content.full_text:
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
428 J Street • Suite 620 • Sacramento, CA 95814-2329
(916) 322-5660 • Fax (916) 322-0886

October 29, 2003

Lawrence R. Allen, District Attorney
Sierra County District Attorney
Post Office Box 457
Downieville, CA 95936

Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. W-03-244

Dear Mr. Allen:

This letter is in response to your request for written advice on the conflict-of-interest provisions of the Political Reform Act.¹ Under California Code of Regulations 18209(8)(B), copy enclosed, unless you are an authorized representative of the official for whom you seek assistance, the Fair Political Practices Commission cannot address your questions or provide analysis. However, we have enclosed same general information that may be helpful ("Can I Vote?") and the Davies Advice Letter, No. A-91-256).²

Moreover, if you believe a violation of the Political Reform Act has occurred, you have the option of filing a complaint with our Enforcement Division.

Should you have additional questions regarding this matter, please do not hesitate to contact me at (916) 322-5660.

Sincerely,

Luisa Menchaca
General Counsel

By: John W. Wallace
Assistant General Counsel

JW:jg
Enclosures

¹ Government Code sections 81000-91014. Commission regulations appear at Title 2, sections 18109-18997, of the California Code of Regulations.
² Note that advice letters are an application of the law to a specific set of facts provided by the requestor. They are not precedential, however, they may be consulted for general guidance. Davies was superseded in 1995 after the personal financial effects test was changed from a $250 effect, to a $250 effect in any 12-month period.

QUESTION: The requestor sought written advice on conflict-of-interest provisions of the Political Reform Act, but the specific legal question cannot be determined from this response letter.

CONCLUSION: The FPPC cannot provide the requested advice because the requestor is not an authorized representative of the official in question, as required by California Code of Regulations 18329(b)(8)(B). General informational materials were enclosed instead.

embedding.summary: FPPC declines to provide conflict-of-interest advice to Lawrence R. Allen (Sierra County District Attorney) because he does not appear to be an authorized representative of the official for whom assistance was requested, per regulatory requirements. General information materials were enclosed.
